channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Industrial sector drags NGX down 0.14%
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s NGX saw a 0.14% dip as industrial sector losses outweighed banking gains. Discover how macroeconomic factors impacted NGX performance this week.
Read More: https://punchng.com/industrial-sector-drags-ngx-down-0-14/

item.title: Privatisation of airports inevitable, says ex-FAAN boss
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former FAAN MD, Dr. Richard Aisuebeogun, states airport privatisation is an economic necessity for Nigeria to build world-class infrastructure and unlock a
Read More: https://punchng.com/privatisation-of-airports-inevitable-says-ex-faan-boss/

item.title: NSCDC busts fake Lagos varsity, rescues 107 students
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NSCDC has uncovered a fake university in Lagos, rescuing 107 students lured into unaccredited programmes and a Ponzi scheme. Learn more about the fake
Read More: https://punchng.com/nscdc-busts-fake-lagos-varsity-rescues-107-students/

item.title: One killed as police rescue 11 abducted travellers in Zamfara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Zamfara State Police Command rescued 11 abducted travellers after a bandit attack on Gummi–Sokoto Road, though one person was killed in the incident.
Read More: https://punchng.com/one-killed-as-police-rescue-11-abducted-travellers-in-zamfara/

item.title: Police arrest 1,198 suspects, rescue 166 kidnap victims — IG
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria Police Force arrests 1,198 suspects for various crimes and rescues 166 kidnap victims in nationwide anti-crime operations. Read more on Punch.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-1198-suspects-rescue-166-kidnap-victims-ig-2/

item.title: Slain NURTW leader laid to rest in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Toba Ajiboye, the slain NURTW organising secretary, was laid to rest in Lagos. Family, friends, and colleagues mourned the union leader’s death.
Read More: https://punchng.com/slain-nurtw-leader-laid-to-rest-in-lagos/

item.title: Edo community seeks intervention after farmer’s death
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Imoga community in Edo State appeals to Governor Okpebholo for intervention after a farmer’s death, citing worsening insecurity and threats to livelihoods.
Read More: https://punchng.com/edo-community-seeks-intervention-after-farmers-death/

item.title: Oil surge boosts revenue amid pipeline security gains
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s oil revenue is soaring due to higher global prices and improved pipeline security. Learn how sustained efforts against oil theft are crucial for
Read More: https://punchng.com/oil-surge-boosts-revenue-amid-pipeline-security-gains/

item.title: PenCom plans dedicated funding for state pension bureaus
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
PenCom plans dedicated funding for state pension bureaus to boost Contributory Pension Scheme compliance nationwide and address poor adoption rates and fin
Read More: https://punchng.com/pencom-plans-dedicated-funding-for-state-pension-bureaus/

item.title: REPORTER’S DIARY: Inside Ghana’s multi-billion-dollar Safari tourism sites
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Explore Ghana’s multi-billion dollar Safari Valley Resort and other tourism sites. Discover sustainable luxury, nature, and healing experiences redefining
Read More: https://punchng.com/reporters-diary-inside-ghanas-multi-billion-dollar-safari-tourism-sites/

item.title: 'I was saving it': Did Littler know sensational nine-darter was coming?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Luke Littler thinks he still has a long way to go to be the "greatest ever" darts player but is motivated to keep checking achievements off his list, starting with going back-to-back at the World Matchplay in Blackpool.

item.title: Buoyed by investors Confidence, Market Capitalisation Crosses to N159trn
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede   The Nigerian stock market continued its positive  run, with stocks repricing across major sectors as investor confidence drove market capitalisation above the N159 trillion mark. The Nigerian Exchange

item.title: Tinubu approves new army battalion for Kwara to strengthen security
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Demola Akinyemi, Ilorin President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has approved the establishment of a new Nigerian Army battalion in Omu-Aran, Irepodun Local Government Area of Kwara State, as part of efforts to strengthen security in the state. The development was disclosed in a statement issued by the Deputy

item.title: Expert Charges Nigerians On Early Retirement Planning
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Ebere Nwoji Pension and retirement planning expert, Mrs Rahinatu Omolamai, has advised Nigerians to embrace early retirement planning as a critical tool for combating old-age poverty.  Omolamai, who is  the

item.title: Bridging Reform Divide With $3.05bn Social Investment
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: James Emejo posits that with the launch of a $3.05 billion World Bank assisted package of programmes focused on poverty reduction and human capital development, the federal government prioritising  is

item.title: Duplo partners Wema Bank to automate financial operations for ALAT business customers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Duplo, a leading financial operating system for African businesses, has partnered with Wema Bank to enable seamless financial management for businesses using the ALAT by Wema Bank platform. Through this partnership, businesses will have access to Duplo’s financial operations platform, enabling them

item.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – US politics live
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: President says 100% tariff would be imposed on generic drugs made overseas after 1 August 2028 ‘for a one year period of time, and 200% thereafter’Sign up for US Breaking News emailUS gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Automo

item.title: Thirty-One Firms Emerge Winners Of 37 Oil, Gas Blocks
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: After a keenly contested bidding process, 31 companies have emerged winners of 37 oil and gas blocks at the 2025 Licensing Round which held in Abuja on Tuesday. THE WHISTLER had reported that a total of 143 companies had submitted 200 bids for 37 out of the 50 oil and gas blocks originally put on [&

item.title: Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation Tasks Students on Saving, Investment Habits
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kemi Olaitan in Ibadan  The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) has called on youths especially students in the country to imbibe the culture of saving, financial discipline, and investment as

item.description: Ebere Nwoji Sovereign Trust Insurance Plc, has reaffirmed its unwavering commitment to delivering on its promise to policyholders through the prompt settlement of genuine claims, maintaining its reputation as a

item.description: Ebere Nwoji  emPLE Nigeria,  one of the licensed insurance underwriting firms, in  partnership with Ecobank Nigeria, has reinforced its commitment to financial inclusion through its  “Protecting Little Dreams” Initiative. According

item.title: Prudential Zenith Congratulates NIA on Investiture of Its Chairman
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Ebere Nwoji Prudential Zenith Life Insurance, has commended the Nigeria Insurers Association (NIA) for achieving the recent  historic milestone of producing its first female President, Mrs Ebelechukwu Nwachukwu, in its 55

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as Traders Weigh Impact of Oil Risks on Rates
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold held a gain as traders monitored threats to energy supply routes that risk stoking inflation and putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates.

item.title: Littler hits nine-darter in epic Matchplay win over Aspinall as MVG beaten
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Luke Littler and Nathan Aspinall produced a World Matchplay epic, with the world No 1 hitting a nine-darter on the way to a mesmerising 11-8 win at the Winter Gardens in Blackpool.

item.title: Rank unveils new community-powered zero-interest capital products after crossing $100 million in user payouts
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Rank, an integrated financial services and wealth management platform, has launched three new community-powered financial services products – Money Circles, Tribe and Perks – designed to dismantle structural barriers to wealth creation for young Africans. By tapping into the trust networks that alre

item.title: Sokoto Govt warns against fertiliser diversion as Kebbe receives 3,600 bags for farmers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Musa Ubandawaki, Sokoto The Sokoto State Government has warned against the diversion, hoarding or resale of fertiliser meant for farmers as the distribution of free agricultural inputs under Governor Ahmed Aliyu’s administration continues across the state. The warning was issued during the distri

item.title: Spiro targets net zero by 2040, sets ESG benchmark for Africa’s electric mobility industry
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: Spiro, Africa’s largest electric mobility company, on Tuesday unveiled its first Sustainability Report, committing to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and read more Spiro targets net zero by 2040, sets ESG benchmark for Africa’s electric mobility industry

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Plus, more ways to lower how much you pay out of pocket for medical care in retirement.

item.title: Innolight Seeks Up to $7 Billion in Biggest Hong Kong Listing in Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s Zhongji Innolight Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing to raise as much as HK$55 billion ($7 billion), the city’s biggest in seven years.

item.title: ASUU Rejects Bayelsa Allowance Package, Insists On CBA Implementation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU), Niger Delta University (NDU) chapter, has rejected the Bayelsa State Government’s recent approval of a 40 per cent allowance package for public servants, insisting that the measure does not address agreements already reached with university lect

item.title: Uwaleke: Stock Market Will Deliver Modest But Attractive Return in H2
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayde Tokede  A renowned capital market economist, Prof Uche Uwaleke, has stated that the Nigerian stock market is expected to deliver a more modest but still attractive return to investors

item.title: Lebanese army deploys in ‘pilot zones’ under US-backed plan
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Lebanese army has begun deploying in three southern towns as part of a US-backed framework agreement with Israel.

item.title: Trump administration attacks US left, alleging Cuba ties: Key takeaways
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US report says the beleaguered government in Havana is the 'connective tissue' of a global 'anti-American coalition'.

item.title: Moldova chooses new prime minister as it pushes for EU membership
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Political newcomer Vasile Tofan inherits Moldova's push to join the European Union by 2030.

item.title: Decomposed body in singer D4vd’s car was ‘unrecognizable’, court hears
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The body of a teenage girl found in the trunk of US singer D4vd's car had been decomposed to the point it was "unrecognizable," a police detective testified in a Los Angeles court on Tuesday. The post Decomposed body in singer D4vd’s car was ‘unrecognizable’, court hears appeared f

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: A trio of Premier League heavyweights all want Marcus Rashford, Arsenal are interested in Aston Villa's Ezri Konsa, and Nottingham Forest look set to sign a new defender.

item.title: Yen Slides Past 163 Mark to Fresh Four-Decade Low Against Dollar
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986, extending its decline and increasingly testing Japanese authorities’ resolve to intervene.

item.title: FMBN’s N500bn recapitalisation ‘ll bridge housing deficit – Gov Sule

item.description: The Nasarawa State Governor, Abdullahi Sule has called on the federal government to actualise the proposed recepitalisation of the Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN) to N500 billion, noting that it will bridge Nigeria’s housing deficit Daily Trust reports that Nigeria has an estimated 28 millio

item.title: SAHCO introduces evacuation chairs
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Skyway Aviation Handling Company (SAHCO) Plc has launched a pilot programme with the acquisition of EVAC+CHAIR emergency evacuation chairs, becoming the first aviation ground handling company in Nigeria to deploy the specialised equipment for passengers with reduced mobility. The investment is aimed

item.title: Why we expanded distribution network to 4000 retail shops — Airtel
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Airtel Nigeria says it has expanded and strengthened its nationwide retail and distribution network to improve customer access to telecommunications services and deepen digital inclusion across the country. The telecommunications company said the move forms part of its continued investment in enhanc

item.title: Police Trust Fund empowers officers’ spouses with vocational skills, others
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Nigerian Police Trust Fund (NPTF) yesterday launched a vocational skills acquisition and empowerment programme for spouses of junior police officers. The programme, according to the NPTF, is aimed at improving the welfare of police families and strengthening the operational effectiveness of the

item.title: Nigeria Congratulates Senegal’s Faye on Election as New ECOWAS Chairman
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigerian government has congratulated Senegal’s President Bassirou Diomaye Faye on his election as the new Chairman of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) Authority of Heads of State and Government. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs, in a statement by its spokesperson, Kimiebi I

item.title: Data: Why we chose transparency over corporate silence – MTN
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: In an era where customer complaints about data consumption often play out on social media, MTN Nigeria took an unusual step. Rather than issue another statement, the company opened its doors and invited critics, customers, influencers and journalists to interrogate its operations at the Data On Tria

item.title: FRC urged to regulate sustainability reporting
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Sustainability Professionals Institute of Nigeria (SPIN) has called on the Financial Reporting Council of Nigeria (FRC) to limit its role to regulating sustainability reporting, warning against positioning itself as the authority on sustainability practice or competing with the professionals it

item.title: France Passes Law Banning Social Media For Children Under 15
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: French lawmakers on Tuesday approved a landmark bill banning children under the age of 15 from using social media platforms, making France the first European Union country to adopt such a restriction. The legislation, which received overwhelming backing in parliament, now awaits President Emmanuel M

item.title: Hearts suffer chastening CL defeat in nightmare first game for Vrancken
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: New Hearts head coach Wouter Vrancken endured a nightmare debut as his side's Champions League hopes all but vanished with a 4-0 defeat against Sturm Graz.

item.title: Climate Crisis Now Human Rights Emergency, NHRC Declares
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has declared climate change a full-blown human rights emergency in Nigeria, warning that the growing toll of floods, droughts, displacement and violent resource conflicts is threatening the fundamental rights of millions of citizens. The Commission said th

item.title: KKR Commercial Real Estate Lender Studies Potential Sale, Merger
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A commercial real estate lender managed by KKR & Co. said it’s considering strategic alternatives that could include a merger or sale of the money-losing company.

item.title: Man Rescued Unhurt From Faulty Elevator On Lagos Island
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: An adult male was rescued unharmed after becoming trapped in a malfunctioning elevator in a three-storey building on Freeman Street, Zapass, Lagos Island East LCDA, on Tuesday The post Man Rescued Unhurt From Faulty Elevator On Lagos Island appeared first on Trending News.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

This was disclosed in a statement signed by the

item.description: The Ukrainian leader announced he was appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place. The post Zelensky Sacks Army Chief Syrsky After Days Of Protests appeared first on Channels Television.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nasarawa State Governor, Abdullahi Sule, has defended President Bola Tinubu’s bid for re-election, insisting that the President’s record in office far surpasses those of other potential presidential contenders ahead of the 2027 general election. item.title: External reserves stand at $52bn – CBN gov, Cardoso
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Yemi Cardoso, says Nigeria’s foreign exchange reserves has presently risen to 52.52 billion dollars. The post External reserves stand at $52bn – CBN gov, Cardoso appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil edged higher after President Donald Trump played down the prospect of near-term talks with Iran while threatening further strikes, as risks to global supply spread beyond the Middle East to the Black Sea.

item.title: Super Micro’s stock soars as its margins unexpectedly double
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The AI server maker now expects gross margins to be in the range of 15% to 17% thanks to an improving customer and product mix

item.title: NDLEA Seizes ₦480bn Drugs, Arrests 77,859
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) says it arrested 77,859 suspected drug traffickers, including 240 major drug barons, and seized illicit drugs worth an estimated ₦480 billion between January 2021 and December 2025. The post NDLEA Seizes ₦480bn Drugs, Arrests 77,859 appeared first on

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Rise on Tech Rally, Oil Gains: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Stocks in Asia were set for a second day of gains as a rebound in chipmakers at the heart of the artificial intelligence boom offset concerns over renewed escalation in the US-Iran conflict.

item.description: The Coalition of Civil Society Organisations for Good Governance has alleged that there is a plot to instigate violence in Osun State ahead of the August 15 governorship election as a pretext for declaring a state of emergency and removing Governor Ademola Adeleke from office. item.title: Draper misses out on US Open main draw - will have to qualify
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Former British No 1 Jack Draper is set to have to qualify for the US Open this year as the 2024 semi-finalist missed out on a spot in the main draw.

item.title: Portugal coach, Jorge Jesus sends message to Ronaldo’s critics
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Portugal’s coach, Jorge Jesus, has dismissed ideas that Cristiano Ronaldo is a problem for the national team, describing the 41-year-old as the symbol for his country. Jesus became the new head coach of Portugal on July 10 with a four-year contract, after the team’s 2026 campaign. The 71

item.title: World Cup: FIFA president, Infantino told to resign after Spain won trophy
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: LaLiga President, Javier Tebas, has urged FIFA President Gianni Infantino to resign following the conclusion of the 2026 tournament. The event wrapped up in New York on Sunday, with Spain securing a 1-0 victory over Argentina, thereby winning their second title. Tebas criticized FIFA’s managem

item.description: Replaced military chief Syrskii clashed with former Defence Minister Fedorov, whose removal last week led to protests.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State, on Tuesday, flagged off the 2026 distribution of fertilisers and other farm inputs to 63,000 registered smallholder farmers in the state. Mbah also said the move was in line with his administration’s commitment to eradicating poverty and growing Enugu

item.title: Hundreds Flee As Wildfire Rips Through Southern France
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The fire swept through wooded hills in the Var region, burning 1,700 hectares (4,200 acres) in just a few hours and prompting the evacuation of nearly 400 people, the area's top official said. The post Hundreds Flee As Wildfire Rips Through Southern France appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: ‘Only cowards play backwards’ — Martinez blasts Argentina after Spain defeat
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina goalkeeper, Emiliano Martinez, warned his teammates that they were guilty of employing cowardly tactics during their World Cup final loss to Spain. Argentina’s journey to a second consecutive final had been marked by an indomitable spirit and an aggressive, high-energy style of play,

item.description: OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. item.title: Show HN: Computable – Buy, sell, and redeem GPU for the exact weeks you want
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Hey we are Computable. We spent years building trading infrastructure at Jump Trading and Coinbase. From that point of view, compute looks like energy markets before 2000: everything trades through private bilateral leases, there’s no visible price, and nothing can be resold. item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: United States Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth, has revealed that the country has spent $37.5 billion on the war involving Iran, according to the latest official estimate presented during a Senate appropriations committee hearing on Tuesday. item.description: Bournemouth reject a £64m bid from Chelsea for Alex Scot after the midfielder turns down a new contract amid growing Premier League interest.

item.title: Nigeria Watched Its Doctors Leave and Is Now Paying the Price
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: When the Nigerian Labour Minister, Chris Ngige, appeared on a popular television show in 2019 and declared that the country had a surplus of doctors, he was not speaking in error or off the cuff. He meant every word. On Channels TV’s Sunrise Daily, he stated that Nigeria had an “excess” of doctors a

item.title: WAFU B U-20 Tourney: Flying Eagles settle in Yamoussoukro ahead Ghana Clash
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria’s Flying Eagles have settled down in Yamoussoukro, Cote d’Ivoire ahead of their opening fixture against Ghana at the WAFU B U20 Championship. The team travelled to Yamoussoukro after arriving in Abidjan on Monday morning, reaching the host city later in the night. The players, an

item.title: EU, Oman sign agreement to establish mission in Muscat
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The European Union and Oman have signed an agreement to open a permanent EU Mission in Muscat, as the regional ​alliance consolidates its footprint in the Gulf area. Vice-President of the European Commission Kaja ​​Kallas and the Omani Foreign Minister ​Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi assented to the lega

item.title: Bauchi gives 1,250 youths N625m start-up grants to boost agric production
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Bauchi State Government has disbursed a total of N625 million in start-up grants to 1,250 youths who recently completed the Agricultural Skills Development Programme as part of efforts to enhance agricultural productivity, create jobs, and promote youth entrepreneurship. The beneficiaries underw

item.title: 2027: North Central Seals Pact With Tinubu, Vows Landslide Victory
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The North Central Agenda (NCA), a leading socio-political advocacy group in the region, has thrown its weight behind President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s re-election bid, declaring that the President’s victory in the North Central during the 2027 presidential election is assured.

item.title: Afreximbank’s $200m bet on Nigerian firm powers Africa’s energy ambitions
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: As African countries intensify efforts to retain more value from their natural resources and build indigenous industrial capacity, the African read more Afreximbank’s $200m bet on Nigerian firm powers Africa’s energy ambitions

item.title: UNICEF urges traditional leaders to report disease outbreaks early
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) has urged traditional and community leaders in Niger State to promptly report suspected public health emergencies to the relevant authorities to strengthen disease prevention, control and outbreak response. Earlier, UNICEF also warned that Niger Stat

item.title: Enzo Fernandez: Argentina played with 'pride' and 'humility'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Enzo Fernandez has praised Argentina for playing with "pride" and "humility" at the 2026 World Cup after it was confirmed FIFA will investigate the team's behaviour after the final.

item.title: Uba Sani: There hasn’t been ethno-religious crisis in Kaduna since I became Governor
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Governor Uba Sani has advised states that want to copy the Kaduna Peace Model, to find out the symptoms and drivers of their peculiar security challenges to enable them succeed. The Governor further said that having done that, the states should also allow the communities to own the Peace Model, beli

item.title: CBN hasn’t said otherwise – Cardoso on validity of N50, N100, other lower naira notes
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria, Olayemi Cardoso, has said that lower naira denominations such as N5, N10, N20, N50, and N100 notes are still legal tender in Nigeria. Cardoso disclosed this while answering questions at the briefing following the 306th Monetary Policy Committee, MPC meeti

item.title: Gemini last models: temperature, top_p, and top_k are deprecated and ignored
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/latest-model Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48998606 Points: 20 # Comments: 4

item.title: PFIPC Probe: Reps Panel Faults MDAs Over Absence, Summons Foreign Affairs Minister, NSA
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The panel described the absence of key government officials as a setback to its ongoing investigation into the activities of the council. The post PFIPC Probe: Reps Panel Faults MDAs Over Absence, Summons Foreign Affairs Minister, NSA appeared first on Channels Television.
